turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Reporting a Lost Item or Filing a Minor Police Report

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, excuse me. I think I lost something.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s okay, take your time. You’ve come to the right place. We deal with lost items all the time. First, I just need to check — do you believe the item was lost, or do you think it might have been stolen?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Lost. I think lost. But I’m not totally sure.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s completely fine. You don’t need to be one hundred percent certain to make a report. We can still record it as lost, and if anything changes later, it can be updated. What item are you missing?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: My backpack. Small one.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, a small backpack. Thank you. Let me enter that. Now, can you tell me when you last clearly remember having it with you?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yesterday. After class.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yesterday after class — that’s helpful. And after class, do you remember where you went? Even roughly is okay.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Library. Then coffee. Then train. Many places.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s okay, that happens a lot. I’ll note multiple possible locations. Now, let’s describe the backpack a bit more so it’s easier to identify if someone hands it in. What colour is it?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Dark blue.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Dark blue, got it. Does it have any logos, patterns, or something attached to it?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes. Keychain. Cartoon face.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s actually very helpful. I’ll include that detail. People often remember keychains more than the bag itself. Now, can you tell me what was inside the backpack?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Laptop. Charger. Notebook. Lunch box.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, laptop, charger, notebook, and a lunch box. Because there’s a laptop inside, it’s especially important that we record this properly. Do you remember the brand or size of the laptop?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Silver one. Medium size. I don’t know brand.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s okay — silver and medium size is still useful. Now, have you already contacted any of the places you visited, like the library or café?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Library, yes. They don’t have it. Café, not yet.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright. I recommend checking with the café later today as well. Sometimes items are found a few hours later. Filing this report now is still the right thing to do. Now I’ll need your name and contact details so we can reach you if the backpack is found.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Daniel Lee. Email is better.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Email is absolutely fine. Please spell it out slowly for me so I can enter it correctly.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: D-A-N-I-E-L dot L-E-E at gmail dot com.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great, thank you. I’ve entered that. Now, just so you know how the process works, if the item is handed in to any police station or transport office, they’ll check the system and contact you using this email.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I also want to let you know that unfortunately not all lost items are recovered, but backpacks with laptops are often returned, especially if they’re left in public places like libraries or trains.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: If I need proof for university… this report is okay?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, exactly. This report can be used as official proof that you reported the loss, which universities usually accept for things like deadline extensions or replacement cards.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. That helps.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good. Now, before I finalise everything, I’ll quickly read the details back to you, just to make sure I’ve recorded it correctly. You lost a small dark blue backpack with a cartoon keychain yesterday after class, possibly at the library, a café, or on the train. Inside were a silver laptop, charger, notebook, and lunch box. Does that sound right?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes. That’s right.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect. I’m finalising the report now. Here is your report number. Please keep it somewhere safe, as you may need it later.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I also strongly recommend cancelling or securing any accounts connected to the laptop, just in case, and checking with the café today.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes. I will.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright. If you remember any new details later — even something small — you can contact us again and update the report.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. Thank you.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re welcome. I hope the backpack turns up soon. Take care.
